title: rochelia disperma var. microcalycina
intro: Rochelia disperma var. microcalycina is a plant species in the family Boraginaceae, commonly known as the "Rock Jasmine" due to its fragrant flowers and ability to thrive in rocky areas. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Rochelia disperma var. microcalycina include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, as well as its ability to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant's flowers and leaves are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally to harness its medicinal benefits.
